description: This figure presents a strain rate–temperature map illustrating the activation domain of discontinuous dynamic recrystallization (DDRX) in Fe–8% Al ferritic steel. The map displays experimental data points plotted against temperature (850–1150°C) on the x-axis and strain rate (0.01–100 s⁻¹, logarithmic scale) on the y-axis. Green circles denote conditions where DDRX is not activated, while red circles indicate DDRX activation. A dashed line separates the two domains, showing that DDRX activates at high temperatures (≥900°C) and high strain rates (≥1–10 s⁻¹), specifically at conditions such as 900°C/10 s⁻¹, 1000°C/10 s⁻¹, and 1100°C/10 s⁻¹. Conversely, DDRX is suppressed at lower strain rates (e.g., 0.1 s⁻¹ at 900–1100°C), where continuous dynamic recrystallization (CDRX) dominates instead. The figure demonstrates the competitive nature between DDRX and CDRX mechanisms as a function of thermomechanical processing parameters, establishing critical processing windows for controlling microstructural evolution in hot-deformed low-density ferritic steels.
